# Real-Time Facades

Sources used: Current Laravel 13.x documentation page for facades.

## Purpose
The docs show real-time facades as a way to treat an application class or interface as if it were a facade while keeping the underlying service resolved from the container.

## Import Rule
The docs state that a real-time facade is generated by prefixing the imported class or interface namespace with `Facades\`.

The documented example uses an interface import changed from `App\Contracts\Publisher` to `Facades\App\Contracts\Publisher`.

## Documented Example
The docs show a model method refactor from method injection to a real-time facade call:

```php
use Facades\App\Contracts\Publisher;
use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model;

class Podcast extends Model
{
    /**
     * Publish the podcast.
     */
    public function publish(): void
    {
        $this->update(['publishing' => now()]);

        Publisher::publish($this);
    }
}
```

The docs say the implementation is resolved from the service container using the portion of the name after the `Facades` prefix.

## Testing Real-Time Facades
The docs show testing a real-time facade with `shouldReceive(...)`:

```php
use App\Models\Podcast;
use Facades\App\Contracts\Publisher;
use Illuminate\Foundation\Testing\RefreshDatabase;

pest()->use(RefreshDatabase::class);

test('podcast can be published', function () {
    $podcast = Podcast::factory()->create();

    Publisher::shouldReceive('publish')->once()->with($podcast);

    $podcast->publish();
});
```

## Boundary
The docs frame real-time facades as preserving testability without passing the dependency manually each time.

## Topics Requiring Fresh Lookup
Run a new documentation lookup before using real-time facades with custom container bindings, package classes, alternate testing styles, or patterns beyond the documented `Facades\` import rule and `shouldReceive(...)` example.
